title: 6.1 Sequential Search
subtitle: Linear search in ordered and unordered lists

content.introduction: Sequential (linear) search scans elements one by one and is the baseline method when no ordering or index structure exists.

sections.title: 6.1.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define linear scan behavior, termination conditions, and best/average/worst-case outcomes.
examples: Search target 42 in an unsorted array
Explain first-match vs all-match scanning variants
Describe failure condition when target is absent

sections.title: 6.1.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Describe implementation for unordered and ordered lists, including early stop optimization on sorted data.
examples: Trace index progression for ordered early-stop search
Return both found flag and position index
Handle empty list and duplicate values

sections.title: 6.1.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Complexity is O(n) time and O(1) space; discuss when this simplicity is acceptable in practice.
examples: Compare linear search with binary search prerequisites
Use linear scan for tiny datasets and one-off checks
Estimate expected comparisons under random target position

keyTakeaways: Understand linear search behavior and limits
Choose linear search for low-overhead simple tasks
